response:
There once was a programmer named Dave,
Who swore his GitHub would behave,
He force-pushed main, made a grave blunder,
That took his whole repo down under,
Now he begs cloud backups to save.

This follows the classic limerick structure of an AABBA rhyme scheme, with playful inside jokes familiar to programmers (the nightmare of misusing force push on the main branch, lost code, and reliance on cloud backups) that still land as lighthearted fun for general audiences.
